1969 Italian film

***Zum zum zum - La canzone che mi passa per la testa*** is 1969 film directed by [Bruno Corbucci](/wiki/Bruno_Corbucci "Bruno Corbucci").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Plot

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Zum_zum_zum_-_La_canzone_che_mi_passa_per_la_testa&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Plot")]

The sons of a music-loving insurance broker take extreme measures to find happiness for themselves and others, with the help of the power of music.

## Cast

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Zum_zum_zum_-_La_canzone_che_mi_passa_per_la_testa&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Cast")]

* [Little Tony](/wiki/Little_Tony_(singer) "Little Tony (singer)") as Tony Bertozzini
* Isabella Savona as Rosalia Caputo
* [Orietta Berti](/wiki/Orietta_Berti "Orietta Berti") as Sister Teresa
* [Peppino De Filippo](/wiki/Peppino_De_Filippo "Peppino De Filippo") as Peppino Bertozzini
* Walter Brugiolo as Carletto Bertozzini
* [Paolo Panelli](/wiki/Paolo_Panelli "Paolo Panelli") as Paolo
* [Nino Terzo](/wiki/Nino_Terzo "Nino Terzo") as Filiberto Caputo
* [Dolores Palumbo](/wiki/Dolores_Palumbo "Dolores Palumbo") as Tosca Bertozzini
* [Vittorio Congia](/wiki/Vittorio_Congia "Vittorio Congia") as Vittorio
* [Enzo Cannavale](/wiki/Enzo_Cannavale "Enzo Cannavale") as Filippo
* [Enrico Montesano](/wiki/Enrico_Montesano "Enrico Montesano") as Enrico aka Camomilla
* [Luca Sportelli](/wiki/Luca_Sportelli "Luca Sportelli") as Sor Ignazio
* Mario Meniconi as Enrico's father
* Enzo Guarini as Luigino Monticelli
* [Giacomo Furia](/wiki/Giacomo_Furia "Giacomo Furia") as Pasquale
* Dante Cleri as florist
* [Lino Banfi](/wiki/Lino_Banfi "Lino Banfi") as doorman
* [Bruno Canfora](/wiki/Bruno_Canfora "Bruno Canfora") as himself
* [Pippo Baudo](/wiki/Pippo_Baudo "Pippo Baudo") as himself
* Daniela Ghibli as herself
* Mirella Pamphili as Mariana
* [Valeria Sabel](/wiki/Valeria_Sabel "Valeria Sabel") as Miss Vincenzi
* [Gianfranco D'Angelo](/wiki/Gianfranco_D%27Angelo "Gianfranco D'Angelo") as party guest

## Sequel

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Zum_zum_zum_-_La_canzone_che_mi_passa_per_la_testa&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Sequel")]

The film was followed by a sequel, *[Zum zum zum n° 2](/wiki/Zum_zum_zum_n%C2%B0_2 "Zum zum zum n° 2")*.

## Response

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Zum_zum_zum_-_La_canzone_che_mi_passa_per_la_testa&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Response")]

An Italian review of the time described the film as ”an unpretentious work, whose thin plot is a mere pretext for the songs performed by the protagonists’”.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## References

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Zum_zum_zum_-_La_canzone_che_mi_passa_per_la_testa&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: References")]

1. **[^](#cite_ref-1)** ["Zum zum zum - La canzone che mi passa per la testa"](https://www.avclub.com/film/reviews/zum-zum-zum-la-canzone-che-mi-passa-per-la-testa-1969). *[The A.V. Club](/wiki/The_A.V._Club "The A.V. Club")*.
2. **[^](#cite_ref-2)** [*Zum zum zum - La canzone che mi passa per la testa*](https://www.cinematografo.it/film/zum-zum-zum-la-canzone-che-mi-passa-per-la-testa-kweg4wmh) (in Italian), retrieved 12 February 2023
